title: Genista tridens
intro: Genista tridens, also known as three-peak broom or three-peak genista, is a perennial legume plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small yellow flowers and thorny stems. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Genista tridens include flavonoids, particularly genistein and luteolin, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant are teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Genista tridens is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Genista, which is a group of shrubs or small trees commonly known as brooms. The genus Genista is further classified within the subfamily Faboideae, which includes a wide range of legume plants. Genista tridens is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found in dry, rocky areas. The classification of Genista tridens reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Fabaceae family.
